JOIN KEY 1/3 — normalized before any join
JOIN KEY 2/3
JOIN KEY 3/3
INEI ubigeo (never merge against ONPE ubigeo)

household weight. NOTE this module's universe is LARGER than sumaria's: 44,599 households in 2025 against sumaria's 33,702. combine() anchors on one module and LEFT-joins the rest so the difference is reported, never silently intersected.
type of dwelling. CODES NOT STABLE: a code 7 exists in 2004 and 2020, code 8 appears in 2025. Check per year before pooling.
predominant wall material. Codes grow over time (8 in 2004, 9 from 2014). New categories appended, not remapped.
predominant floor material. Confirm the category list per year.
predominant roof material. Confirm per year.
rooms in the dwelling. CODES/RANGE NOT STABLE: max 13 in 2004, 15 from 2014.

water supply. CODES NOT STABLE: 1-7 through 2014, a code 8 appears from 2020. Code 1 = public network inside the dwelling, which is the figure INEI headlines (2024: 83.5% official vs 82.5% computed in prior work — a ~1pp definitional gap, so confirm before publishing a level).
lighting. == 1 is ANY electric lighting, which is NOT what INEI headlines: INEI publishes 'red publica' (92.6% in 2024) while any-source gives 96.1%. Label whichever you use.

DO NOT POOL ACROSS 2023. CONFIRMED DEFINITION BREAK, and INEI DOES DOCUMENT IT — in Diccionario_2025.pdf (shipped inside the module-01 zip), where the question reads 'Su hogar tiene: Conexion a Internet (FIJO/MOVIL)?'. The .dta variable label is TRUNCATED to 'su hogar tiene: conexion a internet' and drops the '(fijo/movil)' that reveals the change, so the break is invisible if you only read the .dta metadata — read the Diccionario. The raw series jumps from ~55% (2022) to ~90% (2023+) because mobile access now counts. INEI's published 'hogares con internet' is 58.4% (TIC 4T-2024), a different concept again.

FIXED connection — the variable that RECOVERS the pre-2023 concept (a connection in the home), so a comparable series survives the break instead of being abandoned. 20.7% of households in 2025, and 30.1% OF THOSE WITH ANY INTERNET. NOTE THE NAME: the Diccionario PDF calls it P1144B1 but the .dta column is p114b1 — the shipped documentation and the shipped data disagree on the name.
mobile post-paid access (37.4% of households in 2025). Column is p114b2, NOT p1144b2 as the Diccionario writes it.
mobile pre-paid access (44.0% in 2025) — the single biggest reason p1144 jumped after 2023. Column is p114b3, not p1144b3.

PARTIAL YEAR — INEI's Nota Explicativa (shipped in the zip) states 104b1/104b2 were EXCLUDED from the 2025 questionnaire to cut interview time and only REINCORPORATED from September 2025. The 2025 database therefore covers Sep-Dec ONLY. Do not read a 2025 annual level from it.
PARTIAL YEAR (Sep-Dec 2025) — same Nota Explicativa as p104b1.
household has a mobile phone. 95.1% in 2024 (matches INEI ~95%).
unsatisfied basic need 1 (inadequate dwelling). Confirm INEI's NBI definitions before publishing.
unsatisfied basic need 2 (overcrowding). Confirm before use.
